skill.name: regulatory-affairs-head
skill.description: Senior Regulatory Affairs Manager expertise for HealthTech and MedTech companies. Provides strategic regulatory guidance, submission management, regulatory pathway analysis, global compliance coordination, and cross-functional team leadership. Use for regulatory strategy development, submission planning, regulatory risk assessment, and team coordination activities.
skill.summary: Senior Regulatory Affairs Manager expertise for HealthTech and MedTech companies. Provides strategic...
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from regulatory terminology, file names inside Markdown backticks, and documentation text. No prompt injection, network calls, credential access, or command execution evidence was found. The only confirmed concern is a helper script that writes local JSON status data.

low_findings.title: Local JSON File Persistence
low_findings.description: The helper script writes regulatory submission records to a local JSON file. This is expected tracking behavior, but users should avoid storing sensitive health or commercial data without local access controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is explicit and semantically tied to saving tracker data. It is local persistence, not exfiltration or malware behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive Static Pattern Detections
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, C2, reconnaissance, and external command detections are false positives. The cited content is regulatory guidance, terms such as design controls and monitoring, placeholder asset names, and Markdown inline code for file names.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation and Markdown formatting only. No executable shell invocation, network scanning, C2 behavior, or cryptographic implementation is present at these locations.

content.value_statement: HealthTech and MedTech teams need structured regulatory plans before they commit resources. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code organize FDA and EU MDR pathways, risks, timelines, and stakeholder work.
actual_capabilities: Maps FDA 510(k), PMA, De Novo, and EU MDR submission considerations from included references.
Structures regulatory strategy decisions around impact, stakeholders, implementation, and review.
Helps plan submission timelines, team responsibilities, and approval checkpoints.
Identifies common regulatory risks such as weak documentation, late authority engagement, and insufficient evidence.
Provides a local Python tracker for submission status reports and overdue item review.
limitations: It is not a substitute for qualified regulatory counsel or official authority guidance.
It does not include every country, authority, device type, or current regulatory update.
The tracker stores data locally and does not provide access control or encryption.
Referenced files named in SKILL.md are not all present in this package.

use_cases.title: Select a Submission Pathway
use_cases.description: Compare likely FDA or EU MDR routes and document the assumptions behind a recommended pathway.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Submission Plan
use_cases.description: Turn product details into milestones, evidence needs, owner assignments, and authority interaction steps.

use_cases.title: Review Regulatory Risk
use_cases.description: Assess gaps in clinical evidence, technical documentation, QMS readiness, and post-market obligations.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a Pathway
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a plain-language regulatory route summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize the likely regulatory pathway for a Class II medical device in the United States. Include key assumptions and next questions.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Submission Checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for a product team preparing evidence.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a submission preparation checklist for an FDA 510(k). Include technical documentation, testing, labeling, and quality system tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Market Strategies
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced comparison across United States and European Union launch options.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare FDA 510(k) and EU MDR Class IIa planning for this product. Show timeline risks, evidence needs, and team dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Draft an Executive Risk Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced leadership communication for a regulatory decision meeting.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft an executive regulatory risk brief for a delayed submission. Include impact, root causes, mitigation actions, owners, and escalation triggers.

output_examples.input: We need a 510(k) readiness review for a software-enabled device.
output: Pathway assumptions and predicate device questions.
Readiness checklist covering software documentation, performance testing, labeling, and QMS evidence.
Risk summary with owners for unresolved gaps.

output_examples.input: Our team is planning an EU MDR submission for a Class IIa device.
output: Technical documentation and clinical evidence workstream plan.
Timeline risks for Notified Body engagement and response cycles.

output_examples.input: Create a monthly regulatory status update for leadership.
output: Submission status summary by market and authority.
Recommended escalations and next review dates.

best_practices: Provide device class, intended use, target markets, and current evidence when asking for strategy.
Ask the model to separate assumptions, confirmed facts, and open regulatory questions.
Validate outputs against current authority guidance before using them in formal submissions.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated strategy as legal advice or final authority guidance.
Do not store protected health information or confidential trade secrets in the local tracker.
Do not ask for a universal pathway without device classification, market, and intended use details.

faq.question: Can this skill choose the final regulatory pathway?
faq.answer: It can recommend likely pathways and assumptions. A qualified regulatory professional should confirm final decisions.

faq.question: Does it support both FDA and EU MDR planning?
faq.answer: Yes. The included references cover FDA device pathways and EU MDR submission considerations.

faq.question: Can it create formal submission documents?
faq.answer: It can outline and draft planning content. Final submissions need expert review and current authority requirements.

faq.question: Does the tracker send data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence of network transmission was found. The tracker reads and writes a local JSON file.

faq.question: Is this suitable for protected health information?
faq.answer: No. The package does not provide encryption, access controls, or privacy safeguards for sensitive data.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk level low?
faq.answer: Static alerts were reviewed as false positives, but a local file write exists and should be handled carefully.
